title: asteromoea pygmaea
intro: Asteromoea pygmaea, commonly known as the dwarf evening primrose, is a small flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Asteromoea pygmaea include its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as its ability to support the immune system. In herbal preparations, Asteromoea pygmaea is often made into a tea or tincture, and it is also used in topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
